input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule V.1.4.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.1.4.3
            TITLE: intentional or excessive ground contact of any portion of the vehicle other than the tires will

            CONTENT:
            rule V.1.4.3: Intentional or excessive ground contact of any portion of the vehicle other than the tires will
V.1.4.3 Intentional or excessive ground contact of any portion of the vehicle other than the tires will 
forfeit a run or an entire dynamic event  
The intent is that sliding skirts or other devices that by design, fabrication or as a consequence 
of moving, contact the track surface are prohibited and any unintended contact with the 
ground which causes damage, or in the opinion of the Dynamic Event Officials could result in 
damage to the track, will result in forfeit of a run or an entire dynamic event

final_answer: rule V.1.4.3: Intentional or excessive ground contact of any portion of the vehicle other than the tires will V.1.4.3 Intentional or excessive ground contact of any portion of the vehicle other than the tires will forfeit a run or an entire dynamic event The intent is that sliding skirts or other devices that by design, fabrication or as a consequence of moving, contact the track surface are prohibited and any unintended contact with the ground which causes damage, or in the opinion of the Dynamic Event Officials could result in damage to the track, will result in forfeit of a run or an entire dynamic event
